goal: Make Josh 2.0 Telegram the iPhone-first command center with tap-first controls and clear activity visibility.

aiBotRevolution.source: Telegram Bot API 10.0 / May 2026 AI bot feature release
enabledFirst: Inline Mode readiness for quick-pick status/routing results
Guest Mode readiness for Josh 2.0 and JAIMES
deferred: profile chat automation except for a narrow manual pilot
aiBotRevolution.rule: Use Telegram's new AI bot surfaces to reduce typing and improve visibility, but keep task queues plus Brain Feed as the durable record.

runtimeHooks.josh2: OpenCLAW Telegram inline_query handler installed and gateway restarted.
runtimeHooks.jaimes: Hermes Telegram InlineQueryHandler installed and gateway restarted.
runtimeHooks.jain: OpenCLAW Telegram inline_query handler installed on the shared JAIMES/J.AI.N mini.

inlineMode.rule: Do not run a competing Telegram poller. Existing OpenCLAW/Hermes Telegram gateways call telegram_inline_results.py when they receive inline_query updates.

inlineKeyboards.rule: Use single-column rows for important choices and max two buttons per row for compact secondary actions.

callbackQueries.rule: Every callback must answerCallbackQuery quickly so the Telegram spinner clears.

replyKeyboard.rule: Reserve persistent reply keyboards for a future dedicated control-panel mode. Inline buttons are less intrusive for normal chat.

webApp.rule: Use when Mission Control has a stable HTTPS URL; local file is ready and dashboard-safe.

copyTextButtons.rule: Use for commands, one-time codes, URLs, or handoff snippets that Josh 2.0 may need to paste elsewhere.

forceReply.rule: Use for step-by-step forms when Josh 2.0 must type a specific answer.

editableWorkCards.rule: Use scripts/josh_work_card.py for any user-requested task with more than one step, any task likely to exceed about 60 seconds, or any task that changes Mission Control/agent state. Use simple sections so Josh can immediately see what is happening, which tool is being used, what decision is being made, what evidence exists, and what comes next.
updateRules: Send or edit the work card at task start.
Edit the same card when the active step changes.
Edit the same card when blocked, waiting, done, or handed off.
Write Current step as Action, Tool, and Decision in plain language.
Never show raw shell fragments, jq selectors, JSON snippets, traceback noise, or internal refs unless Josh explicitly asks for raw output.
Use Done so far for short evidence bullets, not a running dump of every tool call.
Prefer inline buttons for common next actions instead of asking Josh 2.0 to type.

fastAcknowledgement.rule: For Josh 2.0 direct Telegram turns, watch OpenCLAW session metadata for prompt.submitted and send an immediate compact work card without polling Telegram or reading message content.
expectedBehavior: OpenCLAW records prompt.submitted for the direct Telegram session.
Fast-ack watcher sends a compact received/working card within a few seconds.
OpenCLAW continues the full GPT/tool response and can update or complete the card.
fastAcknowledgement.privacy: No Telegram message text is read, stored, or published by the watcher.

overviewDigest.rule: Use scripts/josh_telegram_digest.py overview|daily for compact ecosystem summaries.

agentQuickCards.rule: Use scripts/josh_agent_quick_card.py <agent> or agent:<agent> callbacks for compact agent-specific status cards.

modelRouteButtons.defaultPolicy: Lean heavily on Gemini 3 Flash for dashboard-safe first-pass review, summaries, planning, log analysis, and second opinions. Keep Codex/GPT-5.5 for execution, private data, approvals, auth, repo edits, device actions, and final integration. Suggest Grok only when public/X/current-events, market/social narrative, or public sentiment context is central.

buttons.useWhen: dashboard-safe review, summary, long-context read, log analysis, planning, or second opinion
highly relevant public X/current-events/social sentiment/market narrative context
repo edits, private connectors, secrets/auth, device actions, approvals, final integration

rules: Start with a plain headline: Josh 2.0 is working, Josh 2.0 is done, Josh 2.0 needs attention, or Josh 2.0 is paused.
Use separate readable sections for Objective, Current step, Done so far, Issues, and Next steps.
Objective must name the target system, concrete work, and intended outcome; avoid generic placeholders such as Sync agent ecosystem state.
Current step must include Action, Tool, and Decision bullets so Josh can understand the live work without reading logs.
Keep Status, Route, Using, and Updated as a short footer.
Do not duplicate Current step or Next steps inside Done so far.
Translate tool output into human-readable evidence. Do not expose raw command fragments like jq selectors or partial JSON.
Keep each line to one short mobile-readable sentence when possible.
For non-trivial tasks, send an editable work card first and keep that card current instead of sending repeated standalone paragraphs.

Only send the final Complete/What was done/Issues/Appropriate next steps/Approval needed template after all work is actually done, or when a blocker requires Josh's attention. Do not continue routine cleanup after the final template.
Final What was done bullets must be user-facing outcomes and verification, not noisy implementation trivia.
Only create Approval options buttons from real Approval needed bullets. Never create approval buttons from n/a, Context, status metadata, or no-action summaries.
When Codex allowance mode is conserve or exhausted, say that explicitly in the Route bullet and name the fallback provider before doing the work.
Suggest Gemini 3 Flash heavily for dashboard-safe first-pass work before Codex execution.
Suggest Grok only when public X/current-events, market/social narrative, or public sentiment context is central.
When routing to JAIMES, state the target model/auth and why before handoff.
When mentioning the remote Mac/agent, say Josh 2.0. Do not shorten it to Josh in operator-facing Telegram text.
When mentioning JOSHeX, clarify that it is the personal-laptop Codex coordinator lane and not the Josh 2.0 Mac.

If the user mentions cookie.codex, Codex cookies, Keychain Not Found, or a visible keychain alert, treat it as macOS/Codex keychain/device work. Do not treat it as Sorare cookie freshness unless the user explicitly says Sorare.

Mission Control publishing and dashboard regeneration should happen automatically during agent work. Do not show a routine refresh button on default task cards; reserve force-sync controls for diagnostics, stale dashboard recovery, or explicit user requests.
requiredLines: Objective: <target system + concrete work + intended outcome>
Current step: Action: <current action>; Tool: <tool in use>; Decision: <current decision>
statusTemplate.example:
Josh 2.0 is working

Objective:
- Test Telegram readability.

Current step:
- Action: validating the simpler work card format.
- Tool: josh_work_card.py dry run.
- Decision: keep raw command details out of the live card unless requested.

Done so far:
- Brain Feed hook fired.
- Direct chat path works.

Issues:
- None

Next steps:
- No action needed.

Status: Working
Route: Josh 2.0 direct chat; foreground lane.
Using: Codex 5.5 / OpenCLAW session
Updated: 22:25 EDT
